name: Miach
description: Miach was a member of the Tuatha Dé Danann, known for his incredible healing abilities. He was the son of Dian Cécht, the physician of the Tuatha Dé Danann. Miach surpassed his father in skill, most famously by reattaching the severed arm of Nuada, the king, with complete functionality. This act of healing challenged Dian Cécht's authority and pride, leading to tragic consequences. Dian Cécht, consumed by jealousy and resentment, ultimately killed Miach. From Miach's grave, 365 herbs sprang forth, each possessing the power to heal a specific ailment, representing the extent of Miach's unsurpassed medical knowledge. These herbs were then cataloged by his sister, Airmed. Miach represents the pinnacle of healing prowess and the tragic cost of envy and ambition. His story highlights the importance of skill, knowledge, and the potential dangers of unchecked pride. He is a symbol of advanced medical knowledge and the healing power of nature, forever connected to the herbs that sprung from his grave. Miach's legacy continues to inspire those seeking medical knowledge and represents the ideal of a selfless healer, even in the face of mortal danger and familial betrayal.
subtitle: Healer of the Tuatha Dé Danann

keyMyths.title: Nuada's Arm
keyMyths.description: Miach restored Nuada's severed arm, surpassing his father's abilities. This act challenged Dian Cécht's authority and triggered his jealousy.

keyMyths.title: Miach's Murder
keyMyths.description: Dian Cécht, envious of Miach's healing skills, killed him out of spite. The murder was a tragic loss for the Tuatha Dé Danann.

keyMyths.title: Herbs from the Grave
keyMyths.description: From Miach's grave, 365 herbs sprang forth, each capable of healing a specific ailment. Airmed cataloged these herbs, preserving Miach's knowledge.

sources.author: Jeffrey Gantz
